title: A Man Young And Old:- The Friends Of His Youth
content:
Laughter not time destroyed my voice
And put that crack in it,
And when the moon�s pot-bellied
I get a laughing fit,
For that old Madge comes down the lane,
A stone upon her breast,
And a cloak wrapped about the stone,
And she can get no rest
With singing hush and hush-a-bye;
She that has been wild
And barren as a breaking wave
Thinks that the stone�s a child.

And Peter that had great affairs
And was a pushing man
Shrieks, �I am King of the Peacocks,�
And perches on a stone;
And then I laugh till tears run down
And the heart thumps at my side,
Remembering that her shriek was love
And that he shrieks from pride.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Butler Yeats, titled "A Man Young And Old:- The Friends Of His Youth", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
